     200                 :             : 
     201                 :             : // Rosano model: R + (L||R) + (L||(R + 1/sC)) — three series stages
     202                 :             : // Stage 1 (R):   R1 — pure DC loss floor
     203                 :             : // Stage 2 (RL):  L1 || R2 — at DC L shorts→0, at high freq→R2
     204                 :             : // Stage 3 (RLC): L2 || (R3 + 1/(sC1)) — adds C for fitting flexibility
     205                 :             : // x[0]=R1, x[1]=R2, x[2]=L1, x[3]=R3, x[4]=L2, x[5]=C1
     206                 :           0 : double CircuitSimulatorExporter::core_rosano_model(double x[], double frequency) {
     207         [ #  # ]:           0 :     for (int i = 0; i < 6; ++i) {
     208         [ #  # ]:           0 :         if (x[i] <= 0) {
     209                 :           0 :             return 1e30;
     210                 :             :         }
     211                 :             :     }
     212                 :             : 
     213                 :           0 :     double w = 2 * std::numbers::pi * frequency;
     214                 :             : 
     215                 :             :     // Stage 1: pure R
     216                 :           0 :     auto Z1 = std::complex<double>(x[0], 0);
     217                 :             : 
     218                 :             :     // Stage 2: L1 || R2
     219                 :           0 :     auto L1 = std::complex<double>(0, w * x[2]);
     220                 :           0 :     auto R2 = std::complex<double>(x[1], 0);
     221         [ #  # ]:           0 :     auto Z2 = parallel(L1, R2);
     222                 :             : 
     223                 :             :     // Stage 3: L2 || (R3 + 1/(jωC1))
     224                 :           0 :     auto L2 = std::complex<double>(0, w * x[4]);
     225                 :           0 :     auto R3plusC = std::complex<double>(x[3], -1.0 / (w * x[5]));
     226         [ #  # ]:           0 :     auto Z3 = parallel(L2, R3plusC);
     227                 :             : 
     228                 :             :     // Three stages in series
     229                 :           0 :     return (Z1 + Z2 + Z3).real();
     230                 :             : }
     231                 :             : 
     232                 :             : // Fitting function in log space for better convergence across wide dynamic range
     233                 :           0 : void CircuitSimulatorExporter::core_rosano_func(double *p, double *x, int m, int n, void *data) {

     239                 :             :     }
     240                 :           0 : }
     241                 :             : 
     242                 :             : // Rosano winding model: Z = Rdc + sum_k(Rk || jwLk)
     243                 :             : // Each stage is a resistor in parallel with an inductor, chained in series.
     244                 :             : // At DC: all L short → Z = Rdc. At high freq: all L open → Z = Rdc + ΣRk.
     245                 :             : // coefficients: [R1, L1, R2, L2, ..., R6, L6]  (12 unknowns, 6 stages)
     246                 :             : // Rdc is passed separately (pinned to measured DC resistance, not a fitting parameter).
     247                 :           0 : double CircuitSimulatorExporter::winding_rosano_model(double x[], double frequency, double dcResistance) {
     248                 :           0 :     const int nStages = 6;
     249         [ #  # ]:           0 :     for (int i = 0; i < 2 * nStages; ++i)
     250         [ #  # ]:           0 :         if (x[i] <= 0) return 1e30;
     251                 :             : 
     252                 :           0 :     double w = 2 * std::numbers::pi * frequency;
     253                 :           0 :     auto Z = std::complex<double>(dcResistance, 0);
     254         [ #  # ]:           0 :     for (int k = 0; k < nStages; ++k) {
     255                 :           0 :         auto Rk = std::complex<double>(x[2 * k], 0);
     256                 :           0 :         auto Lk = std::complex<double>(0, w * x[2 * k + 1]);
     257         [ #  # ]:           0 :         Z += parallel(Rk, Lk);
     258                 :             :     }
     259                 :           0 :     return Z.real();
     260                 :             : }
     261                 :             :

     684                 :             : 
     685                 :             : // =============================================================================
     686                 :             : // FRACPOLE RL SYNTHESIS (Kundert DD-profile)
     687                 :             : //
     688                 :             : // Converts fitted (h, alpha) into concrete R,L values for a series chain of
     689                 :             : // parallel RL stages that approximate Z_skin(f) = h * (2*pi*f)^alpha.
     690                 :             : // =============================================================================
     691                 :           0 : static std::vector<double> compute_fracpole_rl_coefficients(
     692                 :             :     double h, double alpha, double f0, double f1, double lumpsPerDecade = 1.5)
     693                 :             : {
     694                 :           0 :     double decades = log10(f1 / f0);
     695                 :           0 :     int N = std::max(2, static_cast<int>(std::round(lumpsPerDecade * decades)));
     696                 :             : 
     697                 :           0 :     double omega0 = 2 * std::numbers::pi * f0;
     698                 :           0 :     double omega1 = 2 * std::numbers::pi * f1;
     699                 :           0 :     double sigma = pow(omega1 / omega0, 1.0 / (2.0 * N));
     700                 :             : 
     701                 :             :     // DD profile: poles at omega0 * sigma^(2k), zeros at omega0 * sigma^(2k+1)
     702   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :     std::vector<double> poles(N + 1), zeros(N);
     703         [ #  # ]:           0 :     for (int k = 0; k <= N; ++k)
     704                 :           0 :         poles[k] = omega0 * pow(sigma, 2.0 * k);
     705         [ #  # ]:           0 :     for (int k = 0; k < N; ++k)
     706                 :           0 :         zeros[k] = omega0 * pow(sigma, 2.0 * k + 1);
     707                 :             : 
     708                 :             :     // G(s) = Z_skin(s)/s = h * s^(alpha-1)
     709                 :             :     // Rational approximation: G(s) = K * prod(s+zeros) / prod(s+poles)
     710                 :           0 :     double beta = alpha - 1.0;
     711                 :           0 :     double omega_ref = sqrt(omega0 * omega1);
     712                 :             : 
     713                 :           0 :     double H_num_mag = 1.0, H_den_mag = 1.0;
     714         [ #  # ]:           0 :     for (int j = 0; j < N; ++j)
     715                 :           0 :         H_num_mag *= sqrt(omega_ref * omega_ref + zeros[j] * zeros[j]);
     716         [ #  # ]:           0 :     for (int k = 0; k <= N; ++k)
     717                 :           0 :         H_den_mag *= sqrt(omega_ref * omega_ref + poles[k] * poles[k]);
     718                 :             : 
     719                 :           0 :     double target_mag = h * pow(omega_ref, beta);
     720                 :           0 :     double K = target_mag * H_den_mag / H_num_mag;
     721                 :             : 
     722                 :             :     // Partial fraction expansion: G(s) = sum_k A_k / (s + poles[k])
     723                 :             :     // Z_skin(s) = s * G(s) = sum_k A_k * s / (s + poles[k])
     724                 :             :     // Each term = parallel RL stage: R_k = A_k, L_k = A_k / poles[k]
     725                 :           0 :     std::vector<double> coefficients;
     726         [ #  # ]:           0 :     for (int k = 0; k <= N; ++k) {
     727                 :           0 :         double residue_num = K;
     728         [ #  # ]:           0 :         for (int j = 0; j < N; ++j) {
     729                 :           0 :             residue_num *= (zeros[j] - poles[k]);
     730                 :             :         }
     731                 :           0 :         double residue_den = 1.0;
     732         [ #  # ]:           0 :         for (int m = 0; m <= N; ++m) {
     733         [ #  # ]:           0 :             if (m != k)
     734                 :           0 :                 residue_den *= (poles[m] - poles[k]);
     735                 :             :         }
     736                 :           0 :         double A_k = residue_num / residue_den;
     737                 :             : 
     738         [ #  # ]:           0 :         if (A_k > 1e-30) {
     739                 :           0 :             double R_k = A_k;
     740                 :           0 :             double L_k = A_k / poles[k];
     741         [ #  # ]:           0 :             coefficients.push_back(R_k);

     749                 :             : // Compute fracpole coefficients per winding:

     825                 :             : 
     826                 :             : // =============================================================================
     827                 :             : // MUTUAL RESISTANCE LADDER FITTING
     828                 :             : // Based on Hesterman (2020) "Mutual Resistance" paper.
     829                 :             : //
     830                 :             : // The mutual resistance R_ij represents cross-coupling losses between windings.
     831                 :             : // It can be positive (reduces losses when currents oppose) or negative
     832                 :             : // (increases losses). The sign typically changes with frequency.
     833                 :             : //
     834                 :             : // We fit an R-L ladder network to model the frequency dependence:
     835                 :             : //   R_mutual(f) = R0 + parallel(L1, R1 + parallel(L2, R2 + ...))
     836                 :             : //
     837                 :             : // This is similar to the self-resistance ladder but R0 can be negative.
     838                 :             : // =============================================================================
     839                 :             : 
     840                 :             : // Ladder model for mutual resistance - allows negative values
     841                 :           0 : static double mutual_resistance_ladder_model(double x[], double frequency, double dcResistance) {
     842                 :           0 :     double w = 2 * std::numbers::pi * frequency;
     843                 :           0 :     auto R0 = std::complex<double>(dcResistance);
     844                 :           0 :     auto R1 = std::complex<double>(x[0], 0);
     845                 :           0 :     auto L1 = std::complex<double>(0, w * x[1]);
     846                 :           0 :     auto R2 = std::complex<double>(x[2], 0);
     847                 :           0 :     auto L2 = std::complex<double>(0, w * x[3]);
     848                 :           0 :     auto R3 = std::complex<double>(x[4], 0);
     849                 :           0 :     auto L3 = std::complex<double>(0, w * x[5]);
     850                 :             : 
     851                 :             :     // For mutual resistance, parameters can be negative (except inductances)
     852                 :             :     // Only check inductances are positive
     853   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :     if (x[1] < 0 || x[3] < 0 || x[5] < 0) {
                   #  # ]
     854                 :           0 :         return 1e30;  // Large penalty
     855                 :             :     }
     856                 :             : 
     857   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :     return (R0 + parallel(L1, R1 + parallel(L2, R2 + parallel(L3, R3)))).real();
                   #  # ]
     858                 :             : }
     859                 :             : 
     860                 :             : // Data structure for mutual resistance fitting
     861                 :             : struct MutualResistanceFitData {
     862                 :             :     double dcMutualResistance;
     863                 :             :     std::vector<double> frequencies;
     864                 :             : };
     865                 :             : 
     866                 :           0 : static void mutual_resistance_ladder_func(double *p, double *x, int m, int n, void *data) {
     867                 :           0 :     MutualResistanceFitData* fitData = static_cast<MutualResistanceFitData*>(data);

     869                 :             :     
     870         [ #  # ]:           0 :     for(int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
     871                 :           0 :         x[i] = mutual_resistance_ladder_model(p, fitData->frequencies[i], dcResistance);
     872                 :             :     }
     873                 :           0 : }

    1174                 :             :     std::vector<double> coefficients, std::vector<int> coordinates, std::string name) {
    1175                 :             :     // Rosano topology: Z = R1 + (L1||R2) + (L2||(R3+C1))
    1176                 :             :     // Three stages in series. Each stage is a two-terminal block.
    1177                 :             :     // Layout: each stage occupies a horizontal row.
    1178                 :             :     //   - Stage R: single resistor (horizontal)
    1179                 :             :     //   - Stage RL: L on left rail, R on right rail, both between same top/bottom nodes
    1180                 :             :     //   - Stage RLC: L on left rail, R+C on right rail (R then C vertical)

    1182                 :           0 :     std::vector<ordered_json> deviceJsons;

    1185   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :     if (coefficients.size() < 6) return {deviceJsons, connectorJsons};
    1186                 :             : 
    1187                 :           0 :     double R1 = coefficients[0];
    1188                 :           0 :     double R2 = coefficients[1];
    1189                 :           0 :     double L1 = coefficients[2];
    1190                 :           0 :     double R3 = coefficients[3];
    1191                 :           0 :     double L2 = coefficients[4];
    1192                 :           0 :     double C1 = coefficients[5];
    1193                 :             : 
    1194                 :             :     // We use two vertical rails: left rail (x=coordinates[0]-6) and right rail (x=coordinates[0])
    1195                 :             :     // Components on the left rail are horizontal (angle 180), on the right rail vertical (angle 90)
    1196                 :             :     // Each stage: left component from nodeA to nodeB, right component from nodeA to nodeB (parallel)
    1197                 :             :     // Then a connector links nodeB of this stage to nodeA of the next stage
    1198                 :             : 
    1199                 :           0 :     int x = coordinates[0];

                   #  # ]
    1205         [ #  # ]:           0 :         deviceJsons.push_back(rJson);
    1206                 :           0 :         y -= 6;
    1207                 :             :         // Connector from R1 output to Stage 2 input

    1209                 :           0 :     }
    1210                 :             :

    1213                 :             :         // L1: horizontal on left rail
    1214   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         auto l1Json = create_inductor(L1, {x - 6, y}, 180, name + " Stage2 L");
                   #  # ]

    1216                 :             :         // R2: horizontal on right rail (offset by +6 in x), same y

    1219                 :             :         // Connect top nodes (L1 top to R2 top)

          #  #  #  #  #

    1226                 :           0 :     }
    1227                 :             :

    1230                 :             :         // L2: horizontal on left rail

    1756                 :             : // =============================================================================
    1757                 :             : // FRACPOLE SPICE EMITTERS
    1758                 :             : // =============================================================================
    1759                 :             : 
    1760                 :             : // Emit the fracpole subcircuit + instantiation for winding i in NgSpice/LTSpice format.
    1761                 :             : // The winding resistance model is:
    1762                 :             : //   Rdc in series with the skin_effect subcircuit (fracpole + gyrator).
    1763                 :             : //   P+ ---[Rdc]---[skin_effect]--- Node_R_Lmag
    1764                 :           0 : static std::string emit_fracpole_winding_spice(
    1765                 :             :     const FractionalPoleNetwork& net, size_t windingIndex, const std::string& is,
    1766                 :             :     double dcResistance) {
    1767                 :           0 :     std::string s;
    1768         [ #  # ]:           0 :     const std::string fp_name = "fracpole_w" + is;
    1769         [ #  # ]:           0 :     const std::string sk_name = "skin_effect_w" + is;
    1770                 :           0 :     double coef = net.opts.coef;
    1771                 :             : 
    1772                 :             :     // Fracpole subcircuit definition (R/C stages) - pre-computed values for ngspice compatibility
    1773   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :     s += "* Winding " + is + " skin-effect fracpole (alpha=0.5, "
    1774   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :        + std::to_string(net.stages.size()) + " stages)\n";
             #  #  #  # ]
    1775   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :     s += ".subckt " + fp_name + " p n\n";
                   #  # ]
    1776         [ #  # ]:           0 :     for (size_t k = 0; k < net.stages.size(); ++k) {
    1777         [ #  # ]:           0 :         std::string kk = std::to_string(k + 1);
    1778                 :           0 :         double R_scaled = net.stages[k].R * coef;
    1779                 :           0 :         double C_scaled = net.stages[k].C / coef;
    1780   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         s += "R" + kk + " p " + kk + " " + to_string(R_scaled, 12) + "\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
                   #  # ]
    1781   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         s += "C" + kk + " " + kk + " n " + to_string(C_scaled, 12) + "\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
                   #  # ]
    1782                 :           0 :     }
    1783         [ #  # ]:           0 :     if (net.Cinf > 0.0) {
    1784                 :           0 :         double Cinf_scaled = net.Cinf / coef;
    1785   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         s += "Cinf p n " + to_string(Cinf_scaled, 12) + "\n";
             #  #  #  # ]
    1786                 :             :     }
    1787         [ #  # ]:           0 :     if (net.Rinf > 0.0) {
    1788                 :           0 :         double Rinf_scaled = net.Rinf * coef;
    1789   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         s += "Rinf p n " + to_string(Rinf_scaled, 12) + "\n";
             #  #  #  # ]
    1790                 :             :     }
    1791   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :     s += ".ends " + fp_name + "\n\n";
                   #  # ]
    1792                 :             : 
    1793                 :             :     // Skin-effect subcircuit definition (fracpole + gyrator)
    1794                 :             :     // The gyrator converts impedance fracpole -> admittance fracpole
    1795                 :             :     // so resistance increases with frequency (skin effect)
    1796   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :     s += ".subckt " + sk_name + " 1 2\n";
                   #  # ]
    1797         [ #  # ]:           0 :     s += "* Gyrator: converts impedance fracpole to admittance fracpole\n";
    1798         [ #  # ]:           0 :     s += "Eg1 3 0 1 2 1\n";          // VCVS sense: input port voltage
    1799         [ #  # ]:           0 :     s += "Gg1 1 2 3 0 1\n";          // VCCS: drives port with gyrator current
    1800         [ #  # ]:           0 :     s += "Rg1 3 0 1e12\n";           // High impedance termination (gmin^-1)
    1801   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :     s += "Xfp 3 0 " + fp_name + "\n";
                   #  # ]
    1802   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :     s += ".ends " + sk_name + "\n\n";
                   #  # ]
    1803                 :             : 
    1804                 :             :     // DC resistance element
    1805   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :     s += "Rdc" + is + " P" + is + "+ Node_fp_" + is + " {Rdc_" + is + "_Value}\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
             #  #  #  # ]
    1806                 :             :     // Skin effect instance
    1807   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :     s += "Xskin" + is + " Node_fp_" + is + " Node_R_Lmag_" + is + " " + sk_name + "\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
             #  #  #  # ]
    1808                 :             : 
    1809                 :           0 :     return s;
    1810                 :           0 : }
    1811                 :             : 
    1812                 :             : // Emit the fracpole subcircuit for core losses.
    1813                 :             : // Core loss is represented as a frequency-dependent resistance in parallel
    1814                 :             : // with the magnetizing inductance.
    1815                 :             : // Uses pre-computed values (coef already applied) for ngspice compatibility.
    1816                 :             : //
    1817                 :             : // IMPORTANT: For core losses, we do NOT include Cinf or Rinf terminal elements.
    1818                 :             : // - Cinf would short-circuit the magnetizing inductance at high frequencies
    1819                 :             : // - Rinf would provide a DC path that doesn't exist for core losses
    1820                 :             : // The R-C stages alone provide the frequency-dependent loss behavior we need.
    1821                 :             : // NOTE: emit_fracpole_core_spice is currently unused; kept for reference.
    1822                 :             : #if 0
    1823                 :             : static std::string emit_fracpole_core_spice(
    1824                 :             :     const FractionalPoleNetwork& net, size_t numWindings) {
    1825                 :             :     std::string s;
    1826                 :             :     const std::string fp_name = "fracpole_core";
    1827                 :             :     double coef = net.opts.coef;
    1828                 :             : 
    1829                 :             :     s += "* Core loss fracpole (alpha=" + to_string(net.opts.alpha, 3) + ", "
    1830                 :             :        + std::to_string(net.stages.size()) + " stages)\n";
    1831                 :             :     s += ".subckt " + fp_name + " p n\n";
    1832                 :             :     for (size_t k = 0; k < net.stages.size(); ++k) {
    1833                 :             :         std::string kk = std::to_string(k + 1);
    1834                 :             :         // Apply coef scaling directly to values for ngspice compatibility
    1835                 :             :         double R_scaled = net.stages[k].R * coef;
    1836                 :             :         double C_scaled = net.stages[k].C / coef;
    1837                 :             :         s += "R" + kk + " p " + kk + " " + to_string(R_scaled, 12) + "\n";
    1838                 :             :         s += "C" + kk + " " + kk + " n " + to_string(C_scaled, 12) + "\n";
    1839                 :             :     }
    1840                 :             :     // NOTE: Cinf and Rinf are intentionally omitted for core losses.
    1841                 :             :     // Cinf would short-circuit Lmag at high frequencies, causing simulation failure.
    1842                 :             :     // Rinf would provide an unphysical DC path through the core loss network.
    1843                 :             :     s += ".ends " + fp_name + "\n\n";
    1844                 :             : 
    1845                 :             :     // Instantiate in parallel with the magnetizing inductance node
    1846                 :             :     s += "Xcore_loss Node_R_Lmag_1 P1- " + fp_name + "\n";
    1847                 :             : 
    1848                 :             :     return s;
    1849                 :             : }
    1850                 :             : 
    1851                 :             : #endif
    1852                 :             : // Emit core loss network using R-L ladder topology for SPICE.
    1853                 :             : // The R-L ladder provides impedance that INCREASES with frequency:
    1854                 :             : // - At low frequency: L shorts out R, so total Z is low
    1855                 :             : // - At high frequency: L is open, so R dominates, Z is high
    1856                 :             : // This matches the behavior needed for core losses (higher losses at higher frequency).
    1857                 :             : // Coefficients are [R1, L1, R2, L2, R3, L3] from calculate_core_resistance_coefficients().
    1858                 :           0 : static std::string emit_core_ladder_spice(
    1859                 :             :     const std::vector<double>& coeffs, size_t numWindings) {
    1860                 :             :     
    1861         [ #  # ]:           0 :     if (coeffs.size() < 2) {
    1862         [ #  # ]:           0 :         return "";  // Not enough coefficients
    1863                 :             :     }
    1864                 :             :     
    1865                 :           0 :     std::string s;
    1866   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :     s += "* Core loss R-L ladder (" + std::to_string(coeffs.size()/2) + " stages)\n";
             #  #  #  # ]
    1867                 :             :     
    1868                 :             :     // Build ladder: series of (L || R) stages
    1869                 :             :     // Z = L1 || (R1 + L2 || (R2 + L3 || R3))
    1870                 :             :     // Start from innermost stage and work outward
    1871         [ #  # ]:           0 :     std::string lastNode = "P1-";
    1872                 :           0 :     int stageCount = static_cast<int>(coeffs.size() / 2);
    1873                 :             :     
    1874         [ #  # ]:           0 :     for (int k = stageCount - 1; k >= 0; --k) {
    1875                 :           0 :         double R = coeffs[k * 2];
    1876                 :           0 :         double L = coeffs[k * 2 + 1];
    1877                 :           0 :         std::string kk = std::to_string(k + 1);
    1878   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         std::string thisNode = (k == 0) ? "Node_R_Lmag_1" : ("Node_Rcore_" + std::to_string(k));
          #  #  #  #  #  
             #  #  #  #  
                      # ]
    1879                 :             :         
    1880                 :             :         // Validate values
    1881   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         if (R <= 0 || L <= 0 || R > 1e6 || L > 1) {
             #  #  #  # ]
    1882                 :           0 :             continue;  // Skip invalid stage
    1883                 :             :         }
    1884                 :             :         
    1885                 :             :         // R in series path
    1886   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         s += "Rcore" + kk + " " + thisNode + " Node_Lcore_" + kk + " " + to_string(R, 12) + "\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
                      # ]
    1887                 :             :         // L in parallel with the rest (connects to lastNode)
    1888   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         s += "Lcore" + kk + " " + thisNode + " " + lastNode + " " + to_string(L, 12) + "\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
                      # ]
    1889                 :             :         
    1890         [ #  # ]:           0 :         lastNode = "Node_Lcore_" + kk;
    1891   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :     }
    1892                 :             :     
    1893                 :             :     // Connect the final node to ground (P1-)
    1894   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :     if (lastNode != "P1-") {
    1895   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         s += "Rcore_final " + lastNode + " P1- 1e6\n";  // High resistance to close dangling node without creating a short
                   #  # ]
    1896                 :             :     }
    1897                 :             :     
    1898                 :           0 :     return s;
    1899                 :           0 : }
    1900                 :             : 
    1901                 :             : // Emit core loss network using Rosano topology for SPICE.
    1902                 :             : // Three stages in series from Node_R_Lmag_1 to P1-:
    1903                 :             : //   Stage 1 (R):   R1 — pure resistance
    1904                 :             : //   Stage 2 (RL):  L1 || R2 — inductor in parallel with resistor
    1905                 :             : //   Stage 3 (RLC): L2 || (R3 + C1) — inductor in parallel with resistor-capacitor series
    1906                 :             : // Coefficients are [R1, R2, L1, R3, L2, C1] from calculate_core_resistance_coefficients(ROSANO).
    1907                 :           0 : static std::string emit_core_rosano_spice(
    1908                 :             :     const std::vector<double>& coeffs, size_t numWindings) {
    1909                 :             : 
    1910         [ #  # ]:           0 :     if (coeffs.size() < 6) {
    1911         [ #  # ]:           0 :         return "";
    1912                 :             :     }
    1913                 :             : 
    1914                 :           0 :     double R1 = coeffs[0];
    1915                 :           0 :     double R2 = coeffs[1];
    1916                 :           0 :     double L1 = coeffs[2];
    1917                 :           0 :     double R3 = coeffs[3];
    1918                 :           0 :     double L2 = coeffs[4];
    1919                 :           0 :     double C1 = coeffs[5];
    1920                 :             : 
    1921                 :           0 :     std::string s;
    1922         [ #  # ]:           0 :     s += "* Core loss Rosano network (R + RL + RLC series stages)\n";
    1923                 :             : 
    1924                 :             :     // Stage 1 (R): series R from core node
    1925         [ #  # ]:           0 :     std::string prevNode = "Node_R_Lmag_1";
    1926         [ #  # ]:           0 :     std::string nextNode = "Node_core_s1";
    1927         [ #  # ]:           0 :     if (R1 > 0) {
    1928   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         s += "Rcore_s1 " + prevNode + " " + nextNode + " " + to_string(R1, 12) + "\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
                   #  # ]
    1929         [ #  # ]:           0 :         prevNode = nextNode;
    1930                 :             :     }
    1931                 :             : 
    1932                 :             :     // Stage 2 (RL): L1 || R2 between prevNode and nextNode
    1933         [ #  # ]:           0 :     nextNode = "Node_core_s2";
    1934   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :     if (R2 > 0 && L1 > 0) {
    1935                 :             :         // R2 path
    1936   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         s += "Rcore_s2 " + prevNode + " " + nextNode + " " + to_string(R2, 12) + "\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
                   #  # ]
    1937                 :             :         // L1 in parallel (same two nodes)
    1938   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         s += "Lcore_s2 " + prevNode + " " + nextNode + " " + to_string(L1, 12) + "\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
                   #  # ]
    1939         [ #  # ]:           0 :         prevNode = nextNode;
    1940                 :             :     }
    1941                 :             : 
    1942                 :             :     // Stage 3 (RLC): L2 || (R3 + C1) between prevNode and ground
    1943   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :     if (R3 > 0 && L2 > 0 && C1 > 0) {
                   #  # ]
    1944                 :             :         // L2 path: prevNode to ground
    1945   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         s += "Lcore_s3 " + prevNode + " P1- " + to_string(L2, 12) + "\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
                #  #  # ]
    1946                 :             :         // R3 + C1 path (in series): prevNode → R3 → C1 → ground
    1947   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         s += "Rcore_s3 " + prevNode + " Node_core_s3c " + to_string(R3, 12) + "\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
                #  #  # ]

    1950                 :             :         // If RLC stage invalid, just connect to ground

    1952                 :             :     }

    1954                 :           0 :     return s;
    1955                 :           0 : }
    1956                 :             : 
    1957                 :             : // Emit Rosano winding AC-resistance network for SPICE.
    1958                 :             : // Topology: Z = Rdc + (R1||L1) + (R2||L2) + ... + (R6||L6)  [flat series of parallel R||L cells]
    1959                 :             : // Rdc is emitted separately. Each cell Rk||Lk sits between two successive nodes.
    1960                 :             : // coefficients: [R1, L1, R2, L2, ..., R6, L6]
    1961                 :           0 : static std::string emit_winding_rosano_spice(
    1962                 :             :     const std::vector<double>& coeffs,
    1963                 :             :     const std::string& is,
    1964                 :             :     double dcResistance) {
    1965                 :             : 
    1966   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :     if (coeffs.size() < 2) return "";
    1967                 :             : 
    1968                 :           0 :     size_t nStages = coeffs.size() / 2;
    1969                 :           0 :     std::string s;
    1970   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :     s += "* Rosano winding AC loss network (" + std::to_string(nStages) + " R||L stages)\n";
             #  #  #  # ]
    1971                 :             : 
    1972                 :             :     // Rdc in series (R_0 in Rosano's notation)
    1973   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :     s += "Rdc" + is + " P" + is + "+ Node_Rdc_" + is + " " + to_string(dcResistance, 12) + "\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
                      # ]
    1974                 :             : 
    1975         [ #  # ]:           0 :     std::string prevNode = "Node_Rdc_" + is;
    1976         [ #  # ]:           0 :     for (size_t k = 0; k < nStages; ++k) {
    1977                 :           0 :         double Rk = coeffs[2 * k];
    1978                 :           0 :         double Lk = coeffs[2 * k + 1];
    1979         [ #  # ]:           0 :         std::string ks = std::to_string(k + 1);
    1980   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         std::string nextNode = (k == nStages - 1) ? "Node_R_Lmag_" + is : ("Node_Rw_" + is + "_" + ks);
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
             #  #  #  # ]
    1981                 :             : 
    1982   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :         if (Rk > 0 && Lk > 0) {
    1983   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :             s += "Rw" + is + "_" + ks + " " + prevNode + " " + nextNode + " " + to_string(Rk, 12) + "\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
             #  #  #  #  
                      # ]
    1984   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :             s += "Lw" + is + "_" + ks + " " + prevNode + " " + nextNode + " " + to_string(Lk, 12) + "\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
             #  #  #  #  
                      # ]
    1985                 :             :         } else {
    1986                 :             :             // Degenerate stage: short through
    1987   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :             s += "Rwshort" + is + "_" + ks + " " + prevNode + " " + nextNode + " 1e-9\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
             #  #  #  # ]
    1988                 :             :         }
    1989         [ #  # ]:           0 :         prevNode = nextNode;
    1990                 :           0 :     }
    1991                 :           0 :     return s;
    1992                 :           0 : }
    1993                 :             : 
    1994                 :             : // Emit saturating magnetizing inductance for ngspice using behavioral modeling.
    1995                 :             : // Uses a tanh-based flux linkage model: Lambda(I) = Lambda_sat * tanh(I/I_sat) + L_linear * I
    1996                 :             : // where L_linear accounts for the air gap contribution.
    1997                 :           0 : static std::string emit_saturating_inductor_ngspice(
    1998                 :             :     const SaturationParameters& sat,
    1999                 :             :     const std::string& windingIndex,
    2000                 :             :     const std::string& nodeIn,
    2001                 :             :     const std::string& nodeOut) {
    2002                 :             :     
    2003         [ #  # ]:           0 :     if (!sat.valid) {
    2004         [ #  # ]:           0 :         return "";  // Fall back to linear inductor
    2005                 :             :     }
    2006                 :             :     
    2007                 :           0 :     std::string s;
    2008                 :           0 :     const double mu0 = 4e-7 * M_PI;
    2009                 :             :     
    2010                 :             :     // Calculate saturation current and flux linkage
    2011                 :           0 :     double Isat = sat.Isat();
    2012                 :           0 :     double lambdaSat = sat.fluxLinkageSat();
    2013                 :             :     
    2014                 :             :     // The model uses: V = d(Lambda)/dt where Lambda = Lambda_sat * tanh(I/I_sat) + L_gap * I
    2015                 :             :     // L_gap is the linear component (from air gap), approximately L_mag * (1 - 1/effective_permeability)
    2016                 :             :     // For a typical gapped core, most inductance comes from the gap, so L_gap ≈ L_mag
    2017                 :           0 :     double effectiveMuR = sat.Lmag / (mu0 * sat.primaryTurns * sat.primaryTurns * sat.Ae / sat.le);
    2018                 :           0 :     double gapFactor = 1.0 / effectiveMuR;  // Fraction of reluctance from gap
    2019                 :           0 :     double Lgap = sat.Lmag * gapFactor;  // Linear (gap) component
    2020                 :             :     
    2021                 :             :     // Ensure reasonable values
    2022         [ #  # ]:           0 :     if (Isat < 1e-6) Isat = 1e-6;
    2023         [ #  # ]:           0 :     if (lambdaSat < 1e-9) lambdaSat = 1e-9;
    2024         [ #  # ]:           0 :     if (Lgap < 1e-12) Lgap = sat.Lmag * 0.5;  // Fallback
    2025                 :             :     
    2026   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :     s += "* Saturating magnetizing inductance (winding " + windingIndex + ")\n";
                   #  # ]
    2027   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :     s += "* Bsat=" + to_string(sat.Bsat, 4) + "T, Isat=" + to_string(Isat, 4) + "A\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
             #  #  #  #  
                      # ]
    2028                 :             :     
    2029                 :             :     // ngspice behavioral inductor using flux linkage
    2030                 :             :     // V = dLambda/dt = dLambda/dI * dI/dt
    2031                 :             :     // For Lambda = Lsat*tanh(I/Isat) + Lgap*I:
    2032                 :             :     // dLambda/dI = Lsat/Isat * sech²(I/Isat) + Lgap
    2033                 :             :     //            = Lsat/Isat * (1 - tanh²(I/Isat)) + Lgap
    2034                 :             :     // We use the GVALUE element with Laplace to model this
    2035                 :             :     
    2036                 :             :     // Simpler approach: Use the inductor with polynomial current dependence
    2037                 :             :     // L(I) = L0 / (1 + (I/Isat)^2) gives smooth saturation
    2038                 :           0 :     double L0 = sat.Lmag;
    2039                 :             :     
    2040   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :     s += ".param Lmag_" + windingIndex + "_L0=" + to_string(L0, 12) + "\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
                #  #  # ]
    2041   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :     s += ".param Lmag_" + windingIndex + "_Isat=" + to_string(Isat, 6) + "\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
                #  #  # ]
    2042                 :             :     
    2043                 :             :     // Use behavioral source: V = L(I) * dI/dt
    2044                 :             :     // L(I) = L0 / (1 + (abs(I)/Isat)^2)
    2045                 :             :     // Note: ngspice behavioral sources use I() for current through voltage source
    2046         [ #  # ]:           0 :     std::string senseName = "Vmag_sense_" + windingIndex;
    2047         [ #  # ]:           0 :     std::string fluxName = "Lmag_flux_" + windingIndex;
    2048                 :             :     
    2049                 :             :     // Current sense (zero volt source)
    2050   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :     s += senseName + " " + nodeIn + " " + fluxName + " 0\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
                #  #  # ]
    2051                 :             :     
    2052                 :             :     // Behavioral inductor: V = L(I) * dI/dt
    2053                 :             :     // Using EL (behavioral voltage source) with ddt() function
    2054   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :     s += "BLmag_" + windingIndex + " " + fluxName + " " + nodeOut;
          #  #  #  #  #  
                #  #  # ]
    2055   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :     s += " V='Lmag_" + windingIndex + "_L0/(1+pow(abs(I(" + senseName + "))/Lmag_" + windingIndex + "_Isat,2))*ddt(I(" + senseName + "))'\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
             #  #  #  # ]
    2056                 :             :     
    2057                 :           0 :     return s;
    2058                 :           0 : }
    2059                 :             : 
    2060                 :             : // Emit saturating magnetizing inductance for LTspice using Flux= syntax.
    2061                 :             : // LTspice supports nonlinear inductors with Flux={expression} directly.
    2062                 :           0 : static std::string emit_saturating_inductor_ltspice(
    2063                 :             :     const SaturationParameters& sat,
    2064                 :             :     const std::string& windingIndex,

    2070                 :             :     }
    2071                 :             :

    2074                 :             :

    2078                 :             :

    2081                 :           0 :     double gapFactor = std::min(0.99, 1.0 / effectiveMuR);

    2083                 :             :

             #  #  #  #

    2093   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :     s += ".param Lambda_sat_" + windingIndex + "=" + to_string(lambdaSat, 12) + "\n";
          #  #  #  #  #

          #  #  #  #  #

          #  #  #  #  #

                #  #  # ]

    2104                 :             : 
    2105                 :             : // =============================================================================
    2106                 :             : // MUTUAL RESISTANCE AUXILIARY WINDING NETWORK
    2107                 :             : // Based on Hesterman (2020) "Mutual Resistance" paper.
    2108                 :             : //
    2109                 :             : // Implements the mutual resistance model using auxiliary windings coupled
    2110                 :             : // to the physical windings. Each winding pair has an auxiliary winding
    2111                 :             : // network that models the cross-coupling AC losses.
    2112                 :             : //
    2113                 :             : // Circuit topology for each winding pair (i, j):
    2114                 :             : //   Physical winding i (Lmag_i) is coupled to auxiliary winding LA_ij
    2115                 :             : //   Physical winding j (Lmag_j) is coupled to auxiliary winding LA_ij
    2116                 :             : //   Shunt resistor RA_ij on auxiliary winding dissipates mutual losses
    2117                 :             : //
    2118                 :             : // The coupling coefficients determine how the mutual resistance varies
    2119                 :             : // with frequency based on the fitted R-L ladder coefficients.
    2120                 :             : // =============================================================================
    2121                 :             : 
    2122                 :           0 : static std::string emit_mutual_resistance_network_spice(
    2123                 :             :     const std::vector<CircuitSimulatorExporter::MutualResistanceCoefficients>& mutualCoeffs,
    2124                 :             :     double magnetizingInductance,
    2125                 :             :     size_t numWindings) {
    2126                 :             :     
    2127   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :     if (mutualCoeffs.empty() || numWindings < 2) {
                   #  # ]
    2128         [ #  # ]:           0 :         return "";  // No mutual resistance to model
    2129                 :             :     }
    2130                 :             :     
    2131                 :           0 :     std::string s;
    2132         [ #  # ]:           0 :     s += "\n* ==== MUTUAL RESISTANCE NETWORK ====\n";
    2133         [ #  # ]:           0 :     s += "* Based on Hesterman (2020) - Auxiliary windings for cross-coupling losses\n";
    2134         [ #  # ]:           0 :     s += "* Power: P = R11*I1^2 + R22*I2^2 + 2*R12*I1*I2*cos(theta)\n\n";
    2135                 :             :     
    2136         [ #  # ]:           0 :     for (const auto& mc : mutualCoeffs) {
    2137                 :           0 :         size_t i = mc.windingIndex1 + 1;  // 1-indexed
    2138                 :           0 :         size_t j = mc.windingIndex2 + 1;
    2139   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         std::string ij = std::to_string(i) + std::to_string(j);
                   #  # ]
    2140                 :             :         
    2141                 :             :         // Check if we have valid coefficients
    2142         [ #  # ]:           0 :         if (mc.coefficients.size() < 2) {
    2143                 :           0 :             continue;
    2144                 :             :         }
    2145                 :             :         
    2146                 :             :         // Determine the coupling polarity based on DC mutual resistance sign
    2147                 :             :         // Positive R12: reduces losses when currents oppose (typical transformer)
    2148                 :             :         // Negative R12: increases losses
    2149         [ #  # ]:           0 :         double sign = (mc.dcMutualResistance >= 0) ? 1.0 : -1.0;
    2150                 :             :         
    2151   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         s += "* Mutual resistance R" + ij + " (dc=" + to_string(mc.dcMutualResistance, 6) + " Ohm)\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
                #  #  # ]
    2152                 :             :         
    2153                 :             :         // Create auxiliary winding network with R-L ladder
    2154                 :             :         // The auxiliary winding models the frequency-dependent mutual resistance
    2155                 :             :         // Topology: series of (L || R) stages
    2156                 :           0 :         size_t numStages = mc.coefficients.size() / 2;
    2157                 :             :         
    2158                 :             :         // First, create the auxiliary winding with the same inductance as Lmag
    2159                 :             :         // This auxiliary winding is coupled to both physical windings
    2160   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         s += "LA_" + ij + " Node_LA_" + ij + "_in Node_LA_" + ij + "_out " + to_string(magnetizingInductance, 12) + "\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
                      # ]
    2161                 :             :         
    2162                 :             :         // Add shunt resistor network on auxiliary winding to model losses
    2163   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :         std::string lastNode = "Node_LA_" + ij + "_out";
    2164         [ #  # ]:           0 :         for (size_t stage = 0; stage < numStages; ++stage) {
    2165                 :           0 :             double R = std::abs(mc.coefficients[stage * 2]);
    2166                 :           0 :             double L = mc.coefficients[stage * 2 + 1];
    2167         [ #  # ]:           0 :             std::string sk = std::to_string(stage);
    2168                 :             :             
    2169                 :             :             // Validate values
    2170   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :             if (R <= 0 || R > 1e6 || L <= 0 || L > 1) {
             #  #  #  # ]
    2171                 :           0 :                 continue;
    2172                 :             :             }
    2173                 :             :             
    2174   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :             std::string nodeR = "Node_Rmut_" + ij + "_" + sk;
                   #  # ]
    2175                 :             :             
    2176                 :             :             // L in parallel with R-chain
    2177   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :             s += "Lmut_" + ij + "_" + sk + " " + lastNode + " " + nodeR + " " + to_string(L, 12) + "\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
             #  #  #  #  
                      # ]
    2178                 :             :             // R in series to next stage
    2179         [ #  # ]:           0 :             if (stage == numStages - 1) {
    2180                 :             :                 // Last stage connects to ground
    2181   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :                 s += "Rmut_" + ij + "_" + sk + " " + nodeR + " 0 " + to_string(R, 12) + "\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
                      # ]
    2182                 :             :             } else {
    2183   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :                 s += "Rmut_" + ij + "_" + sk + " " + nodeR + " Node_Rmut_" + ij + "_" + std::to_string(stage + 1) + " " + to_string(R, 12) + "\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
                      # ]
    2184                 :             :             }
    2185                 :             :             
    2186         [ #  # ]:           0 :             lastNode = nodeR;
    2187         [ #  # ]:           0 :         }
    2188                 :             :         
    2189                 :             :         // Connect auxiliary winding input to ground (forms the load)
    2190   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         s += "Rmut_" + ij + "_gnd Node_LA_" + ij + "_in 0 1e9\n";  // High-Z to avoid DC path
          #  #  #  #  #  
                      # ]
    2191                 :             :         
    2192                 :             :         // Coupling coefficients for auxiliary winding
    2193                 :             :         // The auxiliary winding is loosely coupled to both physical windings
    2194                 :             :         // The coupling determines how current in each winding induces loss in the auxiliary
    2195                 :           0 :         double kBase = 0.1;  // Base coupling - small for loose coupling
    2196                 :             :         
    2197                 :             :         // Scale by sqrt of ratio to balance energy transfer
    2198                 :           0 :         double k_i_aux = sign * kBase;
    2199                 :           0 :         double k_j_aux = sign * kBase;
    2200                 :             :         
    2201                 :             :         // Clamp coupling to valid range (-1, 1)
    2202                 :           0 :         k_i_aux = std::max(-0.99, std::min(0.99, k_i_aux));
    2203                 :           0 :         k_j_aux = std::max(-0.99, std::min(0.99, k_j_aux));
    2204                 :             :         
    2205   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         s += "KA_" + std::to_string(i) + "_" + ij + " Lmag_" + std::to_string(i) + " LA_" + ij + " " + to_string(k_i_aux, 6) + "\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
             #  #  #  # ]
    2206   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         s += "KA_" + std::to_string(j) + "_" + ij + " Lmag_" + std::to_string(j) + " LA_" + ij + " " + to_string(k_j_aux, 6) + "\n";
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
          #  #  #  #  #  
             #  #  #  # ]
    2207                 :             :         
    2208         [ #  # ]:           0 :         s += "\n";
    2209         [ #  # ]:           0 :     }
    2210                 :             :     
    2211                 :           0 :     return s;
    2212                 :           0 : }

    2214                 :           0 : std::string CircuitSimulatorExporterNgspiceModel::export_magnetic_as_subcircuit(Magnetic magnetic, double frequency, double temperature, std::optional<std::string> filePathOrFile, CircuitSimulatorExporterCurveFittingModes mode) {
    2215         [ #  # ]:           0 :     std::string headerString = "* Magnetic model made with OpenMagnetics\n";

    2348                 :             : 
    2349                 :             :     // Generate pairwise K statements for magnetic coupling
    2350                 :             :     // ngspice has a bug with 3+ inductor K statements inside subcircuits - the third inductor
    2351                 :             :     // doesn't get the proper hierarchical path prefix. Use pairwise K statements as workaround.
    2352                 :             :     // Each K statement gets a unique name (K12, K13, K23, etc.)
    2353                 :             :     // Use per-pair leakage inductance calculation for accurate coupling coefficients
    2354         [ #  # ]:           0 :     if (numWindings == 2) {
    2355                 :             :         // Simple 2-winding case - use already calculated coupling, capped at 0.98 for stability
    2356         [ #  # ]:           0 :         double k12 = couplingCoeffs.size() > 0 ? std::min(0.98, couplingCoeffs[0]) : 0.98;
    2357   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         circuitString += "K Lmag_1 Lmag_2 " + std::to_string(k12) + "\n";
             #  #  #  # ]
    2358         [ #  # ]:           0 :     } else if (numWindings >= 3) {
    2359                 :             :         // For 3+ windings, calculate coupling for each pair
    2360         [ #  # ]:           0 :         for (size_t i = 0; i < numWindings; i++) {
    2361         [ #  # ]:           0 :             for (size_t j = i + 1; j < numWindings; j++) {
    2362                 :             :                 // Calculate leakage inductance between winding i and j
    2363                 :           0 :                 double leakageIJ = 0.0;
    2364                 :             :                 try {
    2365                 :           0 :                     auto leakageResult = LeakageInductance().calculate_leakage_inductance(
    2366   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :                         magnetic, Defaults().measurementFrequency, i, j);
                   #  # ]
    2367         [ #  # ]:           0 :                     auto leakagePerWinding = leakageResult.get_leakage_inductance_per_winding();
    2368         [ #  # ]:           0 :                     if (leakagePerWinding.size() > 0) {
    2369   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :                         leakageIJ = resolve_dimensional_values(leakagePerWinding[0]);
    2370                 :             :                     }
    2371                 :           0 :                 } catch (...) {
    2372                 :             :                     // Fallback: use high coupling if calculation fails
    2373                 :           0 :                     leakageIJ = magnetizingInductance * 0.02;  // ~99% coupling
    2374         [ -  - ]:           0 :                 }
    2375                 :             :                 
    2376                 :             :                 // Clamp leakage inductance to avoid negative or very low coupling
    2377         [ #  # ]:           0 :                 if (leakageIJ >= magnetizingInductance) {
    2378                 :           0 :                     leakageIJ = magnetizingInductance * 0.1;  // Limit to ~95% coupling minimum
    2379                 :             :                 }
    2380         [ #  # ]:           0 :                 if (leakageIJ < 0) {
    2381                 :           0 :                     leakageIJ = magnetizingInductance * 0.02;  // ~99% coupling
    2382                 :             :                 }
    2383                 :             :                 
    2384                 :           0 :                 double kij = sqrt((magnetizingInductance - leakageIJ) / magnetizingInductance);
    2385                 :             :                 // Ensure coupling is in valid range
    2386                 :             :                 // Cap at 0.98 for numerical stability (matches IDEAL mode default)
    2387                 :           0 :                 kij = std::max(0.5, std::min(0.98, kij));
    2388                 :             :                 
    2389   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :                 std::string kName = "K" + std::to_string(i + 1) + std::to_string(j + 1);

          #  #  #  #  #

    2395                 :             :     // Core losses: frequency-dependent resistance network in parallel with Lmag
    2396                 :             :     // The network impedance must be >> Lmag impedance, otherwise it shorts the magnetizing inductance
    2397         [ #  # ]:           0 :     auto coreLossTopology = static_cast<CoreLossTopology>(settings.get_circuit_simulator_core_loss_topology());
    2398   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :     auto coreResistanceCoefficients = CircuitSimulatorExporter::calculate_core_resistance_coefficients(magnetic, temperature, coreLossTopology);

    2400                 :             :         // Sanity check: core loss impedance at mid-frequency must be > 10x Lmag impedance

    2402                 :           0 :         double fMid = std::sqrt(1000.0 * 300000.0);
    2403                 :           0 :         double wMid = 2 * std::numbers::pi * fMid;

    2417                 :             :         }
    2418                 :             :         // If zCoreLoss <= 10*zLmag, skip core loss network to avoid shorting Lmag

    3334                 :           0 : double CircuitSimulatorExporter::fracpole_model(double x[], int numCoeffs, double frequency, double dcResistance) {

    3343                 :             :     }
    3344                 :           0 :     return Z.real();
    3345                 :             : }

    3435                 :             : // NL5 circuit simulator exporter

    3438                 :             : // ---- NL5 Component builder ----
    3439                 :             : struct Nl5Cmp {
    3440                 :             :     std::string type;      // e.g. "R_R", "L_L", "C_C", "W_W", "W_T2"
    3441                 :             :     int id;
    3442                 :             :     std::string name;
    3443                 :             :     int node0, node1;
    3444                 :             :     int node2 = -1, node3 = -1; // for 4-terminal (K, G)
    3445                 :             :     // Value parameter: for R=r, L=l, C=c, K=k
    3446                 :             :     std::string valParam;  // parameter name ("r","l","c","k")
    3447                 :             :     std::string valStr;    // value as string
    3448                 :             :     // Extra parameters
    3449                 :             :     std::map<std::string, std::string> extra;
    3450                 :             :     // Position
    3451                 :             :     int px = 0, py = 0, angle = 0;
    3452                 :             :     bool flip = false;
    3453                 :             : };
    3454                 :             : 
    3455                 :             : // ---- Engineering notation formatter for NL5 _txt attributes ----
    3456                 :           0 : static std::string nl5_to_eng(double v, int sigfigs = 4) {
    3457   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :     if (v == 0.0) return "0";
    3458                 :           0 :     bool neg = v < 0;
    3459                 :           0 :     double av = std::abs(v);
    3460                 :           0 :     std::string suffix; double scaled;
    3461   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :     if      (av >= 1e12) { scaled = av/1e12; suffix = "T"; }
    3462   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :     else if (av >= 1e9)  { scaled = av/1e9;  suffix = "G"; }
    3463   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :     else if (av >= 1e6)  { scaled = av/1e6;  suffix = "Meg"; }
    3464   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :     else if (av >= 1e3)  { scaled = av/1e3;  suffix = "k"; }
    3465   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :     else if (av >= 1.0)  { scaled = av;       suffix = ""; }

    3516                 :             : // ============================================================================
    3517                 :             : // CircuitSimulatorExporterNl5Model::export_magnetic_as_subcircuit
    3518                 :             : // ============================================================================
    3519                 :             : // Uses NL5 Winding (W) components as documented in NL5 Reference:
    3520                 :             : // - Each winding is a W_Winding component with turns ratio
    3521                 :             : // - All winding "core" pins connect to a common core node
    3522                 :             : // - Magnetizing inductance connects from core node to ground
    3523                 :             : // - Leakage is modeled via series inductors
    3524                 :             : // - DC resistance and AC losses via series R/L/C networks
    3525                 :             : // ============================================================================
    3526                 :           0 : std::string CircuitSimulatorExporterNl5Model::export_magnetic_as_subcircuit(

    3528                 :             :     double frequency,

    3530                 :             :     std::optional<std::string> filePathOrFile,

    3533         [ #  # ]:           0 :     auto coil = magnetic.get_coil();

    3547         [ -  - ]:           0 :         catch (...) {}

    3579                 :             : 
    3580                 :             :     // ---- NL5 XML generation ----
    3581                 :             :     // Node numbering scheme:
    3582                 :             :     //   0          = ground
    3583                 :             :     //   1..N       = winding positive terminal Pi+  (external)
    3584                 :             :     //   N+1..2N    = winding negative terminal Pi-  (external)
    3585                 :             :     //   50         = core node (common for all windings via ideal transformer)
    3586                 :             :     //   100+i      = internal node after Rdc (before ladder/fracpole), winding i
    3587                 :             :     //   200+i      = internal node after ladder/fracpole (before leakage L), winding i
    3588                 :             :     //   250+i      = internal node after leakage (connects to winding), winding i
    3589                 :             :     //   300+i*20+k = internal nodes within fracpole/ladder stages for winding i, stage k
    3590                 :             :     //   900+k      = internal nodes for core loss network
    3591                 :             : 
    3592                 :           0 :     int nextId = 1;   // component id counter
    3593                 :           0 :     std::string cmps; // accumulated <Cmp> XML
    3594                 :             : 
    3595                 :             :     // Core node where all windings connect via ideal transformers
    3596                 :           0 :     const int node_core = 50;
    3597                 :             : 
    3598                 :           0 :     auto add_R = [&](const std::string& name, int n0, int n1, double val, int px, int py, int angle=0) {
    3599   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :         Nl5Cmp c; c.type="R_R"; c.id=nextId++; c.name=name;
    3600   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :         c.node0=n0; c.node1=n1; c.valParam="r"; c.valStr=to_string(val,12);
    3601                 :           0 :         c.px=px; c.py=py; c.angle=angle;
    3602   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :         cmps += nl5_cmp_to_xml(c);
    3603                 :           0 :     };
    3604                 :           0 :     auto add_L = [&](const std::string& name, int n0, int n1, double val, int px, int py, int angle=0) {

    3606   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :         c.node0=n0; c.node1=n1; c.valParam="l"; c.valStr=to_string(val,12);
    3607                 :           0 :         c.px=px; c.py=py; c.angle=angle;
    3608   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :         cmps += nl5_cmp_to_xml(c);
    3609                 :           0 :     };

    3613                 :           0 :         c.px=px; c.py=py; c.angle=angle;
    3614   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :         cmps += nl5_cmp_to_xml(c);
    3615                 :           0 :     };
    3616                 :             :     // NL5 saturating inductor using L_Lsat model
    3617                 :             :     // Parameters: l=initial inductance, Lsat=saturated inductance, Iknee=knee current
    3618                 :           0 :     auto add_Lsat = [&](const std::string& name, int n0, int n1, double L0, double Lsat, double Iknee,
    3619                 :             :                         int px, int py, int angle=0) {
    3620   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :         Nl5Cmp c; c.type="L_Lsat"; c.id=nextId++; c.name=name;
    3621   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :         c.node0=n0; c.node1=n1; c.valParam="l"; c.valStr=to_string(L0, 12);
    3622                 :             :         // Lsat is the saturated (minimum) inductance
    3623   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         c.extra["Lsat"] = to_string(Lsat, 12);
                   #  # ]
    3624   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         c.extra["Lsat_txt"] = nl5_to_eng(Lsat);
                   #  # ]
    3625                 :             :         // Iknee is the current at knee point (where saturation starts)
    3626   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         c.extra["Iknee"] = to_string(Iknee, 6);
                   #  # ]
    3627   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         c.extra["Iknee_txt"] = nl5_to_eng(Iknee);

                   #  # ]
    3629                 :           0 :         c.px=px; c.py=py; c.angle=angle;
    3630   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :         cmps += nl5_cmp_to_xml(c);
    3631                 :           0 :     };
    3632                 :             :     // NL5 Winding component (W_W): ideal transformer with turns ratio
    3633                 :             :     // node0 = winding +, node1 = winding -, node2 = core
    3634                 :           0 :     auto add_Winding = [&](const std::string& name, int n_plus, int n_minus, int n_core,
    3635                 :             :                            double turns, int px, int py) {
    3636   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :         Nl5Cmp c; c.type="W_W"; c.id=nextId++; c.name=name;
    3637                 :           0 :         c.node0=n_plus; c.node1=n_minus; c.node2=n_core;
    3638         [ #  # ]:           0 :         c.valParam="w"; c.valStr=std::to_string(static_cast<int>(std::round(turns)));
    3639   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         c.extra["model"] = "Winding";
                   #  # ]

    3643                 :             : 
    3644                 :             :     // Layout: each winding is 80 units wide, stacked vertically

    3646                 :           0 :         int base_y = static_cast<int>(wi) * 80;

    3648                 :           0 :         std::string ws = std::to_string(is);
    3649                 :             : 
    3650                 :           0 :         int node_Pplus  = static_cast<int>(wi) + 1;           // Pi+  external

    3661   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :             leakageL = resolve_dimensional_values(leakageInductances[wi-1]);
    3662                 :             :             // Scale leakage to this winding's turns
    3663                 :           0 :             double turnsRatio = numTurns / primaryTurns;
    3664                 :           0 :             leakageL *= turnsRatio * turnsRatio;
    3665                 :             :         }
    3666                 :             : 
    3667                 :             :         // Add port labels (external terminals for SubCir pin mapping)

    3670   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         cmps += nl5_label_to_xml(nextId++, "P"+ws+"p", node_Pplus, 0, base_y);
             #  #  #  # ]
    3671   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :         cmps += nl5_label_to_xml(nextId++, "P"+ws+"n", node_Pminus, 180, base_y+40);
             #  #  #  # ]
    3672                 :             : 
    3673                 :             :         // 1. Rdc (DC resistance)
    3674   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :         add_R("Rdc"+ws, node_Pplus, node_after_rdc, Rdc, 20, base_y);
    3675                 :             : 
    3676                 :           0 :         int node_last = node_after_rdc;
    3677                 :             : 
    3678                 :             :         // 2. AC loss network (FRACPOLE or LADDER)

    3680                 :             :             // FRACPOLE: R/C stages

    3690         [ #  # ]:           0 :                       net.stages[k].C / net.opts.coef, px_R+4, base_y+20, 90);
    3691                 :           0 :                 node_last = node_stage;
    3692                 :             :             }
    3693         [ #  # ]:           0 :             if (net.Cinf > 0.0) {

    3696                 :             :             }

    3699                 :             :             // LADDER: R/L stages

    3704                 :           0 :                 int px = 40 + static_cast<int>(k) * 16;
    3705   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :                 add_L("Ll"+ws+"_"+std::to_string(k), node_last, node_stage, c[2*k], px, base_y);

                      # ]
    3706   [ #  #  #  #  :           0 :                 add_R("Rl"+ws+"_"+std::to_string(k), node_stage, 0, c[2*k+1], px+4, base_y+20, 90);

    3707                 :           0 :                 node_last = node_stage;

    3712                 :             :         }
    3713                 :             : 
    3714                 :             :         // 3. Leakage inductance (series with winding)
    3715   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :         if (leakageL > 0.0 && wi > 0) {

    3717                 :           0 :             node_last = node_after_leak;
    3718                 :             :         } else {
    3719                 :             :             // No leakage for primary or if leakage is zero
    3720                 :           0 :             node_after_leak = node_last;
    3721                 :             :         }
    3722                 :             : 
    3723                 :             :         // 4. Winding (ideal transformer to core)
    3724                 :             :         // NL5 Winding: node0=+, node1=-, node2=core
    3725   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :         add_Winding("W"+ws, node_after_leak, node_Pminus, node_core, numTurns, 130, base_y);
    3726                 :           0 :     }
    3727                 :             : 
    3728                 :             :     // 5. Magnetizing inductance (from core to ground)
    3729                 :             :     // This represents the magnetic energy storage in the core
    3730   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :     if (includeSaturation && satParams.valid) {
    3731                 :             :         // Use saturating inductor model
    3732                 :           0 :         const double mu0 = 4e-7 * M_PI;
    3733                 :           0 :         double Isat = satParams.Isat();
    3734                 :             :         // Calculate gap contribution for saturated inductance

    3736                 :           0 :         double gapFactor = std::min(0.99, 1.0 / effectiveMuR);
    3737                 :           0 :         double Lsat = satParams.Lmag * gapFactor;  // Saturated inductance (just the gap)
    3738                 :             :         // Ensure reasonable minimum values
    3739         [ #  # ]:           0 :         if (Isat < 1e-6) Isat = 1e-6;
    3740         [ #  # ]:           0 :         if (Lsat < 1e-12) Lsat = magnetizingInductance * 0.01;
    3741                 :             :         // NL5 Lsat model: L0 is initial inductance, Lsat is saturated inductance, Iknee is knee current
    3742   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :         add_Lsat("Lmag", node_core, 0, magnetizingInductance, Lsat, Isat,
    3743                 :             :                  160, static_cast<int>(numWindings) * 40);
    3744                 :           0 :     } else {
    3745   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :         add_L("Lmag", node_core, 0, magnetizingInductance, 160, static_cast<int>(numWindings) * 40);
    3746                 :             :     }

    3752                 :             :             // Stage 1 (R): R1 series, Stage 2 (RL): L1||R2, Stage 3 (RLC): L2||(R3+C1)
    3753                 :             :             // coefficients: [R1, R2, L1, R3, L2, C1]

    3770                 :             :             }

    3775   [ #  #  #  # ]:           0 :                 add_C("Ccore_s3", 903, 0, C1, 188, y_base + 48);
    3776                 :             :             }
    3777                 :             :         } else {
    3778                 :             :             // Ridley: R-L ladder from node_core to ground

    3786                 :             :                 }
    3787                 :           0 :                 int node_stage = 900 + k;
